imageComparison.summary: The ideal is a full resource-discovery application with navigation, sidebar filters, a grouped chapter/resource table, repository status panels, and widget previews. The current reference preserves that ideal only as a large embedded image inside a generic shell, so the editable mockup has almost none of the page anatomy Limner needs to compare or iterate.

majorDiffAreas.title: Reference is still an embedded image instead of editable page modules
majorDiffAreas.expected: A real HTML/CSS resource finder with top navigation, left learning-goal filters, grouped chapter rows, compact resource badges, a GitHub repository panel, resource coverage summaries, and widget thumbnails.
majorDiffAreas.actual: The editable DOM contains a context header and a single source image frame; the actual resource finder is only visible inside the image asset.
majorDiffAreas.impact: The target cannot be polished, made responsive, audited for text/links, or moved toward production because the meaningful UI is not editable or semantically represented.

evidence.observation: Only five visible text blocks are detected, all from the generic context header or image-frame label rather than the intended resource finder.
The right screenshot shows the ideal page nested below a separate header, proving the current reference depends on the generated image for the real layout.

majorDiffAreas.recommendedChange: Replace the source image frame with editable DOM that recreates the ideal page architecture using verified local book, widget, and repository resource data.

majorDiffAreas.title: Chapter and resource data are absent from the DOM
majorDiffAreas.expected: Representative real chapter rows grouped by Introduction, Part I Mathematical Optimization, Part II Linear Learning, Part III Nonlinear Learning, and Part IV Appendices, with PDF, Notebook, Colab, Exercise, Slides, and GitHub availability states.
majorDiffAreas.actual: No chapter rows, book part headings, resource badges, counts, or repository source link are present as editable text or links.
majorDiffAreas.impact: The mockup cannot prove resource truth or accessibility, and the visual density of the ideal cannot be judged independently from the generated image.

evidence.observation: The page contains only the generic page title, one sentence, and an image element.

majorDiffAreas.recommendedChange: Build the grouped chapter table and badge clusters from verified repository coverage notes instead of copying generated microcopy.

matches: The color direction roughly echoes the ideal with warm paper, deep navy, gold accents, and academic blue.
The selected generated image remains available as visual guidance.
uncertainties: Exact notebook and Colab coverage should remain conservative unless verified directly from the canonical repository.
possibleIntentionalDeviations: The initial shell intentionally preserved the source image for first-pass alignment, but this iteration should move to editable DOM.

nextIteration.focus: Convert the image-backed reference into a real editable resource-discovery mockup.
steps: Replace the source image frame with an HTML/CSS page shell matching the ideal architecture.
Use verified local book cover and widget GIF assets.
Populate representative chapter/resource rows from the canonical repository evidence, keeping unavailable or uncertain resources muted.
avoidForNow: Do not generate new imagery, add stock imagery, or invent university logos, screenshots, or resource availability.

imageComparison.rationale: The biggest visual and product gap is not a small polish issue; it is that the meaningful UI exists only as pixels. Converting the target into editable modules unlocks all later visual, responsive, SEO, and resource-truth verification.

recommendation.topFix: Replace the generated-image frame with editable HTML/CSS modules for the resource finder.
recommendation.why: This creates the page anatomy required by the target contract while preserving the ideal as visual guidance rather than a production asset.
